results.seo_title: ORCA: An End-to-End Interactive Copilot for Optimized Root Cause…
results.meta_description: ORCA is a copilot for end-to-end causal analysis, featuring causal discovery, effect estimation, explainability, and RCA.
results.body_html:
ORCA is a copilot for end-to-end causal analysis, featuring causal discovery, effect estimation, explainability, and RCA.

Causal discovery: ORCA identifies causal relationships between variables. Evaluation and comparison of performance: ORCA evaluates and compares the performance of different causal analysis workflows. Explainability: ORCA provides insights into the causal relationships identified.

BenefitDescription Causal analysis for domain expertsORCA enables domain experts to leverage advances in causal methods. Validation of research resultsORCA provides real-world data for validation, bridging the gap between researchers and practitioners. Improved workflow executionORCA guides users through the most appropriate causal analysis workflow.

Effectiveness ORCA has been shown to be effective across several real-world use-cases, highlighting its potential to bridge the divide between researchers and practitioners in various domains.

source.title: ORCA: An End-to-End Interactive Copilot for Optimized Root Cause Analysis
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new tool called ORCA, an end-to-end interactive copilot for optimized root cause analysis. The system aims to make causal analysis more accessible to domain experts by providing a user-friendly interface and guiding them through the most suitable workflow. ORCA offers features such as causal discovery, effect estimation, explainability, and Root Cause Analysis (RCA), and can generate reports with key metrics and diagrams. It has been tested on several real-world use-cases, demonstrating its effectiveness in this area.

results.seo_title: Google Redesigns Search Box for the First Time in 25 Years
results.meta_description: Google has redesigned its search box for the first time in 25 years, merging AI Overviews and AI Mode into a new multimodal AI search experience.
results.body_html:
Google has redesigned its search box for the first time in 25 years, merging AI Overviews and AI Mode into a new multimodal AI search experience.

The new search box dynamically expands to accommodate longer, more conversational queries. It supports multimodal inputs directly, allowing users to upload images, PDFs, files, and videos, or drag in content from Chrome tabs. The AI-powered query suggestion system helps users formulate complex, nuanced queries.

Architectural Changes Behind the New Search Experience

Unified AI Overviews and AI Mode The new search experience unifies AI Overviews and AI Mode, providing a seamless conversation starter.

Gemini 3.5 Flash The new search experience runs on Gemini 3.5 Flash, a powerful AI model that delivers near-frontier quality at dramatically lower latency.

Impact of the New Search Experience Google's decision to redesign the foundational interface of its most important product did not happen in a vacuum. The company shared a set of usage statistics during the briefing that reveal just how rapidly user behavior is already changing.

source.title: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
source.summary:
For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm.

At its annual I/O developer conference, Google announced a sweeping redesign of the search box itself — the literal text field where billions of queries begin every day — transforming it from a simple keyword input into a dynamic, AI-driven conversation starter that can accept text, images, PDFs, videos, and even open Chrome tabs as inputs. The company is also merging its AI Overviews and AI Mode features into a single, seamless search flow, eliminating the friction that previously forced users to choose between a traditional results page and an AI-forward experience.

Liz Reid, Google's vice president and head of Search, called it "the biggest upgrade to our iconic search box since its debut over 25 years ago" during a press briefing on Monday.

The announcement arrived alongside a blizzard of other news — new Gemini models, a personal AI agent called Spark, an intelligent shopping cart, a reimagined developer platform — but the search box redesign may prove to be the most consequential. It is the clearest signal yet that Google views the future

results.seo_title: How AI Mode is changing the way people search in the U.S.
results.meta_description: Discover how AI Mode is revolutionizing search in the US, enabling users to shift from keywords to natural language queries.
results.body_html:
One year after launch, AI Mode’s users are shifting from keywords to natural language queries.

Key Findings

  • Users are increasingly using natural language queries to search for information on Google.
  • The shift towards natural language queries is driven by the growing demand for more conversational interfaces.
  • AI Mode is playing a significant role in this trend, enabling users to search for information in a more intuitive and user-friendly way.

Benefits for Older Adults

For older adults, AI Mode offers a range of benefits, including improved accessibility and increased independence.

BenefitDescription
Improved AccessibilityAI Mode’s natural language queries make it easier for older adults to search for information on Google.
Increased IndependenceAI Mode enables older adults to stay connected with family and friends, reducing feelings of loneliness and isolation.

Future Directions

Google is committed to continuing to improve AI Mode, incorporating user feedback and emerging technologies to enhance its capabilities.

AI Mode will continue to evolve, enabling users to search for information in a more intuitive and user-friendly way.

source.title: How AI Mode is changing the way people search in the U.S.
source.summary: A graphic features the text "How people are using AI Mode in the U.S." surrounded by colorful, stylized illustrations of a pencil, planet, banana, gift box, cursor, gamepad, and lipstick on a light blue background.

results.seo_title: BigCodeArena: Judging code generations end to end with code executions
results.meta_description: Back to Articles BigCodeArena: Judging code generations end to end with code executions Team Article Published October 7, 2025 Upvote 22 +16 Terry Yue Zhuo…
results.body_html:
Inspired by LMArena for LLMs, we've built a platform that allows anyone to compare code generation models side-by-side, but with a crucial difference: you can actually run the code and see what it produces. Just submit a coding task, watch two different models generate solutions, execute both programs, and vote on which model produced better results. The outcomes are organized into a leaderboard that displays the community's highest-rated models.

Motivation

The field of code generation has long struggled with reliable evaluation methods. Traditional benchmarks like HumanEval test code against predefined test cases, but these represent only a tiny fraction of real-world programming tasks. Human evaluation platforms exist for general chatbots, but they fall short for code: reading raw source code and mentally simulating its execution is cognitively demanding and error-prone, especially for longer programs or complex UI applications.

You ask two AI models to build a responsive photo gallery website. Both generate code that looks syntactically correct. But which one is actually better? Without running the code, it's nearly impossible to tell. One might produce a beautiful, functional grid layout, while the other might have subtle bugs or poor styling that only become apparent when rendered in a browser.

This observation led us to a key insight: execution feedback is essential for humans to judge code quality reliably. That's exactly what BigCodeArena provides.

The BigCodeArena Platform

Real-Time Execution

Every code snippet generated by models is automatically executed in isolated sandbox environments. Whether it's a Python script, a React web app, a PyGame game, or a C++ algorithm, you can see the actual output, not just the source code.

Multi-Language & Framework Support

Interactive Testing

Multi-Turn Conversations

Real programming isn't one-and-done. BigCodeArena supports multi-turn interactions, allowing you to refine requirements, ask for features to be added, or request bug fixes -- just like working with a real coding assistant.

What We've Learned: 5 Months of Community Evaluation

Since launching in February 2025, BigCodeArena has collected over 14,000 conversations from more than 500 unique users, with 4,700+ high-quality preference votes comparing 10 frontier LLMs.

Programming Topics in the Wild

Language and Framework Popularity

Python dominates with over 4,000 conversations, followed by JavaScript/TypeScript (3,359), HTML (1,601), and C++ (642). Among frameworks, direct Python interpreters lead usage (6,000 sessions), with React (2,729), Core Web (1,574), Streamlit (1,254), and PyGame (1,087) also seeing heavy use.

User Interaction Patterns

Most interactions are focused and efficient: 76% of conversations consist of just 2 turns (one request, one response), with a mean conversation length of 4.12 messages. However, the platform supports extended multi-turn debugging sessions when needed, with some conversations exceeding 10 turns as users refine complex applications.

Model Rankings from Community Votes

From our 14K conversations, we filtered for high-quality pairwise comparisons: conversations with at least two turns and actual code execution. This yielded 4,731 voting samples, with each evaluated model receiving at least 700 votes. We aggregate these votes into Elo ratings using the Bradley-Terry model, which estimates the probability that one model beats another based on head-to-head comparisons.

To ensure robust rankings, we use 100 bootstrap resamples to construct 95% confidence intervals, so we can identify statistically significant performance differences between models.

Environment Matched: Only compares models when both were executed in the same sandbox (e.g., both in React or both in PyGame)

Top Tier: o3-mini and o1-mini consistently lead with the highest Elo ratings and tight confidence intervals. These models maintain top performance regardless of environment or language constraints, showing strong robustness across coding scenarios. Claude-3.5-Sonnet follows closely, particularly excelling when language is controlled.

Mid Tier: GPT-4o, o1, and Gemini-2.0-Pro/Flash form a competitive middle tier. GPT-4o shows some sensitivity to language matching, suggesting room for improvement in multilingual consistency.

Open Source Models: Qwen2.5 variants and Llama-3.3-70B lag behind frontier proprietary models, highlighting the performance gap that remains between leading closed and open models.

Figure: Overall win rate heatmaps (percentage of all pairwise comparisons won) of each model in the sessions across languages (left) and execution environments (right). For each category, we only keep models that appear in at least 3 conversation sessions.

Performance Across Languages

source.title: BigCodeArena: Judging code generations end to end with code executions
source.summary: BigCodeArena: Judging code generations end to end with code executions
